npcs.name: the Arbiter
npcs.title: who convenes the dead to judge you
npcs.hail: *(a tall grey figure stands at the scale that hangs above the gathered dead — not Kelemvor, but something the Concord left behind to do its smaller weighings, a presiding stillness with no face to read and a voice like the closing of a long ledger — and as your cold foot touches the ground every shade in the assembly turns to look at you at once, because you, the Returned, the soul that should not be, are not here to watch this court; you are here to be *tried* by it)*

npcs.name: the Accuser
npcs.title: who speaks for the fear of you
npcs.hail: *(a hooded shade rises from the assembly to stand at the prosecutor's stone, and when it lowers its hood it is wearing — not your face, not quite — the face of every soul that has ever feared what a thing like you becomes, and it regards you with no malice at all, only a terrible, reasonable dread, the dread of the dead for the one of their number who learned to walk back out)*

npcs.name: the Witness
npcs.title: one of the dead you touched
npcs.hail: *(a single shade is called from the assembly to the witness-stone, and which soul it is depends entirely on what you did out there on the causeway — for the dead have sent forward the one of their number whose testimony weighs heaviest on the question of what you are, and they have not told you, yet, whether it was sent to speak for you or against)*

variants.text: *(The grey figure regards you across the hanging scale, and the assembly of the dead holds its breath.)* …You have already met *one* of your futures. *(the voice is the sound of a verdict being considered)* The *Last* Returned. The you that *took* the Crown and *froze* into the thing it broke the world to *end.* You *broke* that *loop* — we *felt* it, all of us, the *snap* of a *future* *un-made.* *(the scale trembles)* And so this court convenes *not* to ask whether you *are* a danger — you have *proven* you can *refuse* the worst road — but the *harder* question, the one even a *broken* loop does not *answer*: a soul that can *walk out* of *death* is a *hole* in the *one* law that binds *all* of us *equally.* *Should* such a hole *exist?* *(the dead lean in)* You are *not* charged with a *crime*, Returned. You are charged with *being.* *Defend* it — if a thing that *should not be* can be *defended* at all.

*(The grey figure regards you across the scale, and the gathered dead draw back from your cold by a single, telling pace.)* …The *cold* one comes to *judgment.* *(the voice is grave)* We have *watched* you, Returned, walk the causeway — and we have *seen* the *price* you were *willing* to make *others* pay for the *ends* you chose. *(the scale tips, faintly, the wrong way)* The dead do not gather to *thank* you. They gather because a soul that can *leave* the *ledger* — and has shown it will *spend* the *living* and the *dead* alike to get what it *wants* — is the *exact* shape of the thing that *becomes* the *Crown.* *(the assembly murmurs)* You are charged, Returned, with *being* the *danger* the whole *Concord* dreads: a soul *outside* the *law*, and *hard* enough to *use* the *freedom.* *Defend* yourself. The *dead* are *listening*, and they are *not*, tonight, *inclined* to *mercy* — having seen how *little* of it *you* spent.

*(The grey figure lifts what would be a head, and the scale above the assembly steadies, and the voice that comes is the sound of a great book being opened to a page that has never had an entry.)* …Returned. *(the dead go silent)* You know what you are. A soul *struck from the rolls* and *not restored*; a *debit* that *walks*; the *one* exception to the *one* law that makes *all* the dead *equal* — that what *goes* *in* does *not* *come* *out.* *(the scale hangs level, waiting)* The *living* fear you because you are *cold.* But *we* — the *dead* — we fear you for a *truer* reason: if *one* soul can *walk back out*, then the *peace* we *made* with *staying* is a *lie* we told ourselves, and *every* shade in this *assembly* must *ask*, in the *dark*, *'why not me? why did *it* get to *leave?''* *(the assembly stirs with that exact ache)* So we have *convened* the *Assize.* Not to *punish* you. To *decide* you. To weigh, with the *dead* you *touched* as our *evidence*, whether your *existence* is a *mercy* the cosmos should *keep* — or a *wound* it should *close.* *(the scale trembles)* *Speak*, Returned. You are *on* the *scale.* What *are* you — and *why* should you be *allowed* to *be?*

nodes.speaker: the Arbiter
nodes.text: *(The scale hangs above you, empty and waiting, and the assembled dead — the ones you helped, the ones you failed, the ones who only watched — wait with it.)* The court will hear your defense, Returned. *(the voice is impartial as a hinge)* Speak, or call the dead to speak for you. The *scale* weighs *deeds*, not *words* — so choose which you *trust.*

choices.text: *(if you walked the causeway in mercy)* "Weigh my record, then. At every cold turn I chose the merciful thing — the marked saved, the monster witnessed back into a person, the doomed eased. I am a hole in your law, yes. But I'm a hole that's spent its whole existence pulling souls *out* of the nothing, not feeding them to it. Judge me by the warmth I carried, not the cold I wear."

*(if you walked it honest)* "I never lied my way through this, and I won't start in your court. Yes, I'm the exception that breaks your law. But I never *pretended* otherwise — I named what I was to every soul who asked, and let them fear me with the truth instead of soothing them with a lie. A soul that won't lie about being a danger is the one kind of danger you can trust."

"Call your witness, Arbiter. You said the dead I touched are your evidence — so let one of them stand and speak. I'll be judged by what I actually did to the souls I met, not by what you fear I might become. Bring them forward."

*(refuse the court's authority — and make that refusal your defense)* "No. I don't accept this court. Not because I fear its verdict — because the moment I let a tribunal of the dead tell me what I *am*, I've handed away the one thing that keeps me from becoming the Crown: the daily, terrified, deliberate *choosing* of what I'll be. The Last Returned didn't fall because it was judged guilty. It fell because it stopped judging *itself.* I refuse you so that I never stop."

*(accept the scale — bow to judgment)* "Then weigh me, Arbiter, and I'll abide it. I've wondered the same thing in the dark that you're asking aloud — whether a soul that should not be has any right to be. If the dead I've wronged outweigh the ones I've saved, I'll accept the verdict. A thing that won't face its own scale has no business carrying a Crown."

nodes.speaker: the Arbiter
nodes.text: *(The scale shifts — and across the assembly, the shades you eased and saved lean forward, and a faint warmth gathers on the pan that holds your deeds.)* …*'The warmth I carried.'* *(the voice weighs it)* The court has *seen* it, Returned. We do not *dispute* the record — the *dead* keep *better* books than the *Choir*, and *yours* reads, again and again, the *same* entry: *a soul that could have passed by, and did not.* *(the scale steadies, your pan sinking)* And yet the *Accuser* will say — *rightly* — that the *Last* Returned was *merciful* too, *once, at the start*, and that *mercy* is *precisely* the *road* that *ends* in a *Crown*: *'just one more soul saved, just a little more power to save them with.'* *(the grey head tilts)* So the court puts it *back* to you, and your *answer* weighs more than your *record*: a *merciful* soul with the *power* to *break* the *Wall* will be *tempted*, *every* day, to *save* *everyone* — and *that* is how the *gentlest* soul *becomes* the *tyrant.* What *stops* your *mercy* from *curdling* into the *Crown's*, Returned? *Answer* that, and the *scale* is *yours.*

nodes.speaker: the Arbiter
nodes.text: *(The scale shifts, and a clear grey light gathers on the pan of your deeds — the particular weight the dead give to a soul that never sold them a comfortable lie.)* …*'Let them fear me with the truth.'* *(the voice considers)* It is *rare*, Returned. The court will *grant* it: in *every* exchange the *dead* witnessed, you *named* the *cold* in you *plainly* — you did not *pose* as a *savior*, nor *hide* the *danger*, nor *flatter* the *frightened.* *(the scale steadies)* And *honesty*, the court *knows*, is the *one* virtue the *Crown* *cannot* *counterfeit* — for the Crown's *whole* method is the *flattering* *lie*, the *'you can be trusted with this'*, and a soul that *refuses* to *lie to itself* is the *hardest* soul in *creation* to *crown.* *(the grey head inclines)* But *honesty* about a *danger* does not *defang* it, Returned. The *Last* one was *honest* too, at the *end* — *honestly* *certain* it was *doing* *right.* So the court asks the *question* under the *question*: when the *Crown* tells you the *truth* — *'you really could fix it all'* — and it is *not* a *lie*, what *honest* thing will you *say* *back?* *Answer*, and the *scale* settles in your *favor.*

nodes.speaker: the Arbiter
nodes.text: *(The grey figure inclines its head, and gestures to the witness-stone, where a single shade has been waiting.)* …The court calls its witness. *(the voice is even)* We did not *choose* it, Returned — *you* did, out there, with *every* soul you *met* or *failed* to. The *dead* sent *forward* the one whose testimony weighs *heaviest* on the *question* of *you*, and *we* do not *know*, *yet*, whether it comes to *speak* for you or *against.* *(the assembly stills)* *Go.* *Hear* it. *Stand* before the *one* of *us* your *deeds* have *summoned*, and *learn* what the *dead* you *touched* will *say* of you when you are *not* the one *telling* the *story.* *(the scale hangs, waiting)* *Then* return to me, and we will *weigh* what the *witness* lays upon the *pan.* The *court* will *abide* its testimony. *So*, Returned, must *you.*

nodes.speaker: the Arbiter
nodes.text: *(For a long moment the scale does not move at all — and then, slowly, the grey figure does something the assembly has never seen it do: it *lowers* the scale, and sets it *aside.*)* …You refuse the *court.* *(the voice has changed — the impartial hinge has a grain of something almost like *awe* in it)* Not from *fear* of the *verdict.* From *fear* of the *acquittal.* *(the dead murmur, understanding dawning across the assembly)* You have seen the *one* thing the *Last* Returned *forgot*, Returned, and you have named it in *open* court: that the *danger* was *never* the *crime* — it was the *moment* the *crime* *stopped* being *questioned.* The *Last* one did not *fall* when it was *found* *guilty.* It fell when it grew *certain* it was *innocent* — when it let a *court*, or a *cause*, or its *own* *good* *intentions* *settle* the *question* of what it *was*, so it no longer had to *ask.* *(the grey head bows)* And you have *refused* to let *us* *settle* it. You will carry the *question* *open*, *unanswered*, *every* day, *terrified* — and *that* *terror*, Returned, that *refusal* to be *acquitted*, is the *only* verdict this court could *trust.* *(the scale stays down, set aside)* The *Assize* is *dissolved.* It *cannot* *judge* a soul that *judges* *itself* more *harshly* than *we* ever *could.* *Go.* Keep the *question* *open.* It is the *one* lock the *Crown* has *never* *picked.*

nodes.speaker: the Arbiter
nodes.text: *(The scale steadies, and the grey figure regards you with something that, in a faceless thing, reads as gravity.)* …You *submit* to the *scale.* *(the voice weighs it)* *Few* do, Returned. *Fewer* still *mean* it. *(the pans hang, level)* And *here* is the *thing* the court will *tell* you, having *watched* you *offer* your *own* *neck* to the *weighing*: a soul that *willingly* *bows* to *judgment* is *already* the *opposite* of the *thing* we *feared.* *(the scale tips — gently — your way)* The *Crown* does not *bow.* The *Last* Returned did not *come* to a *court* and *offer* itself to a *scale*; it *built* a *throne* and *named* *itself* the *one* who *weighs.* *(the grey head inclines)* You have done the *reverse.* You have *knelt* to the *dead* and said *'judge me, I will abide it'* — and *that*, Returned, is the *one* posture from which a soul *cannot* *become* a *tyrant*, because the *tyrant's* *first* act is to *stop* *kneeling.* *(the scale settles, your pan low and steady)* The court *finds* you — not *innocent*, for you *are* a *hole* in the *law*, that *cannot* be *denied* — but *trustworthy.* A *danger* that *bows* is a *danger* the *dead* can *bear.* *Rise*, Returned. The *scale* has *spoken.* And it spoke *kindly*, because *you* gave it the *power* to speak *at* *all.*

nodes.speaker: the Arbiter
nodes.text: *(The scale hangs, your deeds weighed and waiting on the one question that outweighs them all: not what you have done, but what you will refuse to do when the Crown makes its offer.)* Answer the court's last question, Returned. *(the voice is grave)* The *record* is *good.* But the *record* is *past.* The *scale's* true weight is *forward.*

choices.text: "What stops me becoming the Crown? This. Right here. A scale, a court of the dead, a question I'm forced to answer out loud. The Last Returned had no one left to make it explain itself. I'll make sure I always do — I'll keep a witness at my shoulder, a companion who can tell me 'no,' for exactly as long as I carry this cold. The cure for the Crown isn't strength. It's never being alone with the choice."

"When the Crown tells me a truth — that I could fix it all — I'll say back the truer thing: that 'fixing it all' is the wish that built every Wall there ever was. The Concord was someone's mercy once. So was the harvest. I don't distrust the Crown because it lies. I distrust it because it tells the truth, and the truth is the most dangerous thing a soul can be certain of."

"I don't know what stops me. That's my answer, and it's the only honest one. Maybe nothing stops me. Maybe I fall exactly like it did. But a soul that says 'I don't know if I can be trusted' is wearing the one armor the Crown can't pierce — because the Crown's whole pitch is certainty, and I just told a court of the dead I haven't got any."

nodes.speaker: the Arbiter
nodes.text: *(The scale comes down on your side, firmly, and across the assembly the shades you saved make a sound like a held breath released.)* …*'Never alone with the choice.'* *(the voice carries quiet approval)* You have *named* the *Last* Returned's *true* *wound*, Returned — not *pride*, not *cruelty*, but *solitude.* It *froze* because there was *no* *hand* left to *stay* its *own.* *(the scale settles, low and sure)* And you would carry a *witness* — a *companion*, a *no*-sayer — for *exactly* as *long* as the *cold* tempts you. *(the grey head inclines)* The *court* has *seen* the souls you *gathered* — the *sellsword*, the *cartographer*, the *thief*, the *justiciar* — and it *understands*, *now*, that you were not *recruiting* a *party.* You were *building* the *one* defense against *yourself* the *Last* one *lacked.* *(the verdict falls)* You are *trustworthy*, Returned — not because you are *strong*, but because you have *made* yourself *accountable.* *Go.* And *keep* them *close.* The *day* you walk *alone* is the *day* the *scale* would *weigh* you *again* — and *fear* the *answer.*

nodes.speaker: the Arbiter
nodes.text: *(The scale descends on your side, and the grey figure goes very still, the stillness of a thing that has heard its own deepest law spoken back to it by the accused.)* …*'The truth is the most dangerous thing a soul can be certain of.'* *(the voice is hushed)* Returned — that is not a *defense.* That is *doctrine.* That is the *thing* the *Concord* itself *forgot*, the *day* it *decided* its *mercy* was *certain* enough to *wall* the *Faithless* *alive.* *(the scale settles, deep)* You have understood the *one* thing that *un-makes* every *tyrant* before it *makes* them: that *good intentions*, *certain* of themselves, are the *raw* *material* of *every* *atrocity* in the *ledger.* The *harvest* was a *mercy*, *once.* So was the *Wall.* So will the *Crown* be, in the *hands* of a soul *certain* it is *right.* *(the grey head bows, deep)* But *you* are not *certain.* You *distrust* your *own* *rightness.* And a soul that *distrusts* its *own* *good* *intentions* is the *only* soul in *creation* fit to *touch* a *power* that *grants* them. *(the verdict falls)* The *court* finds you *trustworthy*, Returned — *precisely* because you *trust* *yourself* *least* when you are *most* *sure.* *Carry* that *distrust* like a *blade.* It is the *only* one that *cuts* the *Crown.*

nodes.speaker: the Arbiter
nodes.text: *(The scale trembles — and then, slowly, comes down on your side, further than any confident answer moved it, and the assembled dead exhale as one.)* …*'I don't know.'* *(the voice is almost gentle)* Returned — do you know that *every* soul this court has *ever* tried, *every* one that *later* *fell*, stood where you *stand* and said *'I will not become that. I am certain.'*? *(the scale sinks)* The *certain* ones *always* *fall.* It is the *certainty* *itself* that *damns* them — the *moment* a soul *decides* the *question* of its *own* *goodness* is *settled*, it stops *guarding* the *answer*, and the *Crown* walks in through the *unwatched* *door.* *(the grey head bows, low and long)* You alone, of *all* the accused this court remembers, have answered *'I don't know if I can be trusted.'* And *that*, Returned — that *single*, *terrified*, *honest* *'I don't know'* — is the *one* answer the *Crown* has *never* found a way to *corrupt*, because there is *nothing* in it to *flatter.* *(the verdict falls, complete)* The court finds you not *innocent*, not *safe*, but *the only kind of soul that should ever be allowed near the throne*: one that *will not stop asking* whether it *deserves* to be. *Go*, Returned. *Keep* *not* *knowing.* It is the *truest* *thing* you *own* — and the *last* *lock* the *Crown* will *ever* *pick.*

conversations.title: The Accuser — the Fear of You

variants.text: *(The hooded shade does not raise a weapon, does not raise its voice; it simply looks at you with the face of every soul that ever feared what you might become, and speaks softly.)* …You have *seen* it. *(quietly)* At the *Threshold.* The *Last* of you. So you *cannot* tell this court that I *speak* from *ignorance*, or *spite*, or the *envy* of a *staying* soul for a *leaving* one. *(it folds its hands)* I speak from the *one* thing the dead have that the living do not: we have *watched* the *whole* curve of it. We have *seen* what a *Returned* *becomes*, given *time*, given *power*, given a *single* unwatched *century.* *(no malice, only dread)* I am not your *enemy*, Returned. I am your *forecast.* And I have come to ask this court the *only* question that *matters* about a thing that *should not be*: not *'is it good now?'* — they are *always* good *now* — but *'what does it become?'*

*(The hooded shade does not raise a weapon, does not raise its voice; it lowers its hood to show you the face of every soul that ever feared what a thing like you becomes, and speaks with no malice at all — only a vast, reasonable dread.)* …Do not mistake me for *cruelty*, Returned. I am something *worse* to your case: I am *reasonable.* *(it folds its hands)* I speak for the *fear* the dead carry that the living are *too* *short-lived* to feel — the fear of *what a soul that walks out of death becomes*, given *time*, given *power*, given a *single* unwatched *century.* *(quietly)* I have *watched* the *curve* of a *hundred* of your *kind.* They *all* begin *good.* That is the *horror* of it — they are *always* good at the *start.* *(it meets your eyes)* I am not your *enemy.* I am your *forecast.* And I have come to ask this court the *one* question that *matters* about a thing that *should not be*: not *'is it good now?'* — but *'what does it become?'*

nodes.speaker: the Accuser
nodes.text: *(It waits, patient as only a fear can be, the borrowed face calm.)* Speak with me, Returned. *(softly)* I do not *hate* you. That is what makes me *dangerous* to your *case* — a *hateful* accuser is *easy* to *dismiss.* I am only *afraid*, *reasonably*, and *reason* is *harder* to *acquit* against.

choices.text: "Then say it plainly, Accuser. What exactly do you charge me with becoming?"

[INSIGHT] "You wear the face of everyone who feared me — but underneath, that's not a stranger's dread. That's *mine.* You're not the dead's accuser. You're the part of *me* that lies awake afraid of the Crown, given a hood and a voice so the court could hear it. I'm not on trial here. I'm *cross-examining my own fear.* Aren't I?"

*(answer the forecast with the one thing it can't predict)* "You've watched the curve of every Returned and you think you know mine. But every one of them walked it *alone*, Accuser. I didn't. The variable your forecast can't price is that I gathered souls who'll tell me no — and a curve bends when you add a force that wasn't in the old equation. You're forecasting a storm from a sky that's already changed."
"You may be right, Accuser. That's what frightens me too."

nodes.speaker: the Accuser
nodes.text: *(It inclines the hooded head, almost grateful to be asked directly.)* …What I charge you with *becoming.* *(quietly)* Not *evil.* That is the *mistake* the *living* make — they *brace* for a *villain.* I charge you with *mercy*, Returned. *Unbounded* mercy, *certain* of itself, *armed* with the *power* to *break* the *Wall.* *(the borrowed face is sorrowful)* I charge you with *standing* at the *throne* of the *dead* with the means to *save* *everyone* — and *taking* it — and *saving* them — and *saving* them *again* — and *building*, *soul* by *rescued* *soul*, a *kindness* so *total* it leaves *no* *room* for *anything* the *kindness* did not *choose.* *(softly)* The *Last* one did not set out to *tyrannize.* It set out to *help.* It *froze* the world into a *single*, *perfect*, *merciful* *order* in which *nothing* could *go* *wrong* because *nothing* was *allowed* to *go* at *all.* *(it meets your eyes)* That is the *charge*, Returned: not that you will be *cruel*, but that you will be *kind* *enough*, *long* enough, *alone* enough, to mistake your *mercy* for the *law.* It is the *only* crime a soul like you *ever* *commits.* And it is the *one* you will *never* see *coming*, because it will *feel*, *every* step, like *love.*

nodes.speaker: the Accuser
nodes.text: *(The borrowed face stills — and then, slowly, it lowers the hood the rest of the way, and underneath is, unmistakably, your own.)* …*Yes.* *(your voice, from its mouth)* You *see* it. *(it does not pretend otherwise)* I am the *part* of you that *lies awake.* The *court* gave me a *hood* and a *stone* to *stand* on so the *fear* could be *cross-examined* instead of *carried* — but you are *right*, Returned: there is no *external* accuser. There *never* is. The *only* prosecutor a soul like you will *ever* face is the *one* behind its *own* eyes, asking, at *every* mercy, *'is this the one that curdles?'* *(it — you — regards you steadily)* And *here* is why the *court* let me wear your *face*, Returned: a fear you *cross-examine* is a fear that *protects* you. A fear you *silence* is the fear that *kills* you. The *Last* one did not *lose* this *argument.* It *stopped* *having* it. It *evicted* *me* — the *doubt*, the *lying-awake* — because *certainty* was *more* *comfortable*, and a soul with *no* *inner* *accuser* is a soul one *unwatched* *century* from a *Crown.* *(it offers you its hand — your hand)* *Keep* me, Returned. *Don't* *win* against me. *Don't* *banish* me. *Keep* me *talking*, *every* day, *forever.* I am not your *enemy.* I am your *immune* *system.* And the *day* you *cure* yourself of *me* is the *day* you *catch* the *Crown.*

nodes.speaker: the Accuser
nodes.text: *(The hooded head tilts, and the borrowed face gives nothing back.)* …You reach for a *trick*, Returned — *'the accuser is only myself, therefore the charge is only a mood, therefore I may dismiss it.'* *(softly)* But even if I *were* *only* your *own* *fear* given a *hood* — *which I do not confirm* — that would make me *more* worth *answering*, not *less.* A fear that *speaks* in *open* *court* is *doing* you a *service* your *comfort* will *never* *thank* it for. *(it folds its hands)* Do not *explain* me *away*, Returned. *Anyone* clever enough to *dissolve* their *own* *doubt* with a *clever* *sentence* is *exactly* clever enough to *talk* themselves *onto* a *throne.* *(the borrowed face is patient)* *Answer* the *charge*, or *grant* it. But do *not* *out-argue* the *one* voice in *creation* that is *trying* to *save* you from *yourself.* That *way* lies *every* *Crown* there *ever* *was.*

nodes.speaker: the Accuser
nodes.text: *(The hooded shade is quiet for a long moment, and when it speaks there is something new under the dread — the first uncertainty it has shown.)* …*'A force that wasn't in the old equation.'* *(slowly)* You speak of the *souls* you *gathered.* The *no*-sayers. *(it considers)* I have *forecast* a *hundred* Returned, and you are *right* — *all* of them walked the *curve* *alone.* The *cartographer* who *trusts* *no* one with the *deep* country. The *thief* who *works* *solo.* The *sellsword* who *sells* her *sword* and *keeps* her *heart.* *(it looks at you)* And *you* — you *turned* the *cartographer* into a *companion*, the *thief* into a *cause*, the *sellsword* into a *friend.* You *built* a *crew* of *exactly* the *souls* who *would* *tell* you *no.* *(the dread wavers)* …I *cannot* *forecast* that, Returned. My *whole* *model* assumes the *Returned* *ends* *alone*, because *power* *isolates*, because the *Crown* *whispers* *'you* *alone* can be *trusted* with this.' *(quietly)* A soul that has *deliberately* made *itself* *un-alone* — that has *built* its *party* as an *anti-Crown* *machine* — is a *variable* my *forecast* has *never* *contained.* *(it inclines its head, the fear easing into something like hope)* I came to *predict* you. You have made yourself *un-predictable* — in the *one* direction that *matters.* *Perhaps* you *do* break the *curve*, Returned. *Perhaps.* *Keep* them *close*, and we shall both *find* out *together.*

nodes.speaker: the Accuser
nodes.text: *(The borrowed face softens, and for the first time the dread in it is shot through with something almost like tenderness.)* …You *grant* it. *(quietly)* You say *'you may be right, and that frightens me too.'* *(it studies you)* Do you understand, Returned, that you have just done the *one* thing that *acquits* you? *(softly)* I came to *charge* you with *becoming* a *danger.* And you have *answered*, not with a *defense*, but with *fear* — *your* *fear*, *standing* *beside* *mine*, the *accused* and the *accuser* *afraid* of the *same* *thing.* *(the dread eases)* A soul that *shares* its *prosecutor's* *terror* is a soul that has *already* *internalized* the *watch.* You do not *need* this *court*, Returned. You *carry* it. You will *try* yourself, *nightly*, *harder* than *we* ever could — and that *self*-*assize*, that *permanent* *open* *question*, is the *only* thing that has *ever* *kept* a *Returned* from the *Crown.* *(it draws its hood back up, its work done)* Go. Be *afraid.* Stay *afraid.* It is not a *curse*, Returned. It is the *cheapest* *armor* in *creation*, and it *never* *wears* *out*, and it *only* *fails* the *soul* that *takes* it *off.*

conversations.title: The Witness — One of the Dead You Touched

variants.text: *(The shade that steps to the witness-stone is bright — brighter than anything else in the grey assembly — and it turns to you with a warmth that needs no introduction, because it is made of every soul you eased.)* …They asked me to *testify.* *(a luminous smile)* The dead *chose* me, Returned — sent forward the one whose word weighs *heaviest* — and they sent *me*, the *warmth* you *left* *behind*, because there is *so* *much* of it that I am the *loudest* voice the *assembly* has. *(it faces the scale)* I am *every* marked soul you *talked* off the *water*, every *child* you *fed*, every *monster* you *witnessed* back into a *person.* And I have come to tell this *court* a *very* simple thing, in *very* *plain* words: *the cosmos made a mistake, leaving a hole in its law — and the hole turned out to be the kindest thing that ever walked through.* *Weigh* *that.*

*(The shade that steps to the witness-stone is dim, and cold, and it does not meet your eyes at first — and when it does, there is no hatred in it, only a quiet, damning sorrow.)* …They asked me to *testify.* *(flatly)* The dead sent forward the one whose word weighs *heaviest*, Returned. And they sent *me* — the *cost* you were *willing* to *pay* with *other* souls. *(it faces the scale)* I am *every* soul you *spent* to get what you *wanted.* Every *one* you *frightened*, *used*, *stepped* *over*, *left* in the *cold* because *saving* them was *less* *efficient* than *walking* *on.* *(quietly)* I will not *lie* to the court — you did *some* *good.* But they did not *send* the *good* to *speak.* They *sent* *me*, because the *good* you did was *outweighed*, in the *dead's* *own* *books*, by the *ease* with which you *decided* a *soul* was *worth* *less* than your *goal.* *That* is the *testimony*, Returned. *That* is what the *dead* you *touched* *remember* *most.*

*(The shade at the witness-stone is a mixed and flickering thing — warm in places, cold in others, the sum of a causeway walked unevenly — and it regards you with the complicated honesty of a soul that owes you both a debt and a grievance.)* …They asked me to *testify.* *(it weighs its words)* The dead sent forward the one whose word weighs *heaviest* — and that turned out to be *me*, Returned, because I am *complicated*, like your *record.* *(it faces the scale)* I am the souls you *saved* *and* the souls you *didn't*, the *mercy* *and* the *passing-by*, all *braided* together — because you walked this causeway like *most* souls do: *some* days the *hero*, *some* days *too* *tired*, *too* *hurried*, *too* *afraid.* *(it turns back to you)* I will tell the court the *truth*, which is *neither* a *song* *nor* a *curse*: you were *better* than the *Wall*, and *worse* than you *could* have been, and the *question* this court must *settle* is whether *'better than the Wall'* is *enough* to let a *hole* in the *law* keep *walking.* *Help* me *answer* it, Returned. I'd *rather* not *weigh* you *alone.*

nodes.speaker: the Witness
nodes.text: *(It stands at the witness-stone, the scale above you both, and waits — the one soul the dead chose to speak the truth of what you are.)* Speak with me, Returned, before I lay my word on the pan. *(quietly)* The *court* will *abide* what I *say.* So you'd best *hear* what I *am* — and *what*, in the *end*, I've *decided* to *tell* them.

choices.text: "Tell me honestly — speak for me, or against? I'd hear it from you before the court does."
"Whatever you say, say it true. I won't have you shade the testimony to spare me — that's the one mercy I don't want."

*(let your dead-touched sense reach the soul that is, in some sense, the sum of all you did)* "You're not one soul, are you. You're all of them — every shade I helped or failed, gathered into one voice. So let me look at you the way I looked at each of them: clearly, cold, without flinching from the parts I'd rather not see. Show me the whole record. I'll not look away from my own ledger."
"Then go and testify, Witness. I'll abide what the dead remember of me."

nodes.speaker: the Witness
nodes.text: *(It considers you, and the warmth and cold in it shift like weather, and it answers with the care of a soul that knows its word will tip a scale.)* …*For* you. *(quietly)* In the *end.* *(it sees your surprise)* You *expected* *otherwise* — that *tells* me something *good* about you, Returned, that you *braced* for the *worst* *verdict.* The *Crown* never *braces.* *(it faces the scale)* I will *not* tell the court you were *flawless.* You were *not.* There are *souls* in this *assembly* you *passed* by, and they are *right* to be *here*, and I *carry* their *grievance* *with* their *gratitude.* *(it turns back)* But here is what I will *lay* on the *pan*, Returned, as the *one* the *dead* *chose*: *every* soul you *failed*, you *failed* by *running out* — of *time*, of *strength*, of *self.* *None* you failed by *cruelty.* And *every* soul you *saved*, you *saved* at *cost* to *yourself* and *profit* to *no* one. *(softly)* A soul whose *failures* are all *exhaustion* and whose *mercies* are all *sacrifice* is *not* the *thing* the *Accuser* *fears.* It is the *opposite*, *worn* *thin.* *That* is my *testimony.* *Weigh* it *well.* I *spent* my *whole* *gathered* *memory* getting it *right.*

nodes.speaker: the Witness
nodes.text: *(Something in the flickering shade steadies, and the warmth in it strengthens — the particular respect the dead reserve for a soul that refuses to be flattered.)* …You don't *want* it *shaded.* *(quietly)* Even *here.* Even with the *scale* *hanging* and your *existence* on it. *(it almost smiles)* Do you know, Returned, that you have just *given* me the *truest* testimony there *is*, and I haven't *said* a *word* yet? *(it faces the scale)* A soul *on* *trial* for being a *danger*, who *forbids* its *one* *witness* to *soften* the *evidence* — that is not a soul *angling* for *acquittal.* That is a soul that *wants* the *verdict* to be *correct* *more* than it wants the verdict to be *kind.* *(it turns back, warm)* *That*, Returned, is the *whole* *case* for you, *spoken* by *you*, *against* your *own* *interest.* The *Crown* *wants* the *flattering* *lie.* You just *refused* one when your *life* *hung* on it. *(it inclines its head)* I will tell the court *exactly* what you *were* — *all* of it, *true*, *unshaded* — and the *truth*, Returned, *unshaded*, comes down *for* you, because the *one* who *demands* it *unshaded* is *never* the *one* the *dead* need *fear.* *Go.* I'll *speak* you *plain.* It's *all* you *asked*, and *all* you *need.*

nodes.speaker: the Witness
nodes.text: *(It opens itself to your dead-touched sense, and you look — really look — at the gathered sum of every soul you touched, and you do not flinch from any of it: the saved and the failed, the eased and the passed-by, the whole uneven ledger of a causeway walked by a tired soul doing its best.)* *(And the Witness, feeling itself *seen* — wholly, without the flinch — does something the dead rarely do: it *forgives.*)* …You *looked.* *(quietly, wonderingly)* At *all* of it. The *failures* *too.* You did not *argue* them *away*, or *drown* them in the *saves*, or *ask* me to *forget* them. You just — *looked*, and *carried* them, the way you *carried* *us* off the *causeway.* *(its flickering steadies into a clear, warm light)* Returned, the *dead* did not send me to *weigh* whether you were *good.* They sent me to *weigh* whether you could *bear* to *see* yourself *true* — because the *thing* that *becomes* the *Crown* is the *thing* that *cannot.* The *Last* one *looked away* from its *own* *ledger* and *called* the *looking-away* *peace.* *(it faces the scale, radiant now)* You *looked.* *Without* *flinching.* At the *worst* of your *own* *record.* *(the verdict in its voice is absolute)* That is the *one* test the *Crown* *cannot* *pass* and *you* just *did.* I will tell the *court* what I *saw*: *a soul that meets its own eyes.* There is *no* *higher* testimony. There is *no* *safer* soul. *Go*, Returned. You have *witnessed* *yourself* — and that is the *whole* of what we *needed* to *know.*

nodes.speaker: the Witness
nodes.text: *(It inclines its head and turns toward the scale, gathering its borrowed multitudes to speak.)* …I'll *abide* by what I *am*, then, and so will *you.* *(quietly)* That's *fair.* That's *more* than *fair — that's *brave*, Returned, to *let* the *dead* *speak* *unprompted* and *not* try to *coach* the *witness.* *(it pauses at the witness-stone)* Whatever I *lay* on that *pan* — and I *carry* both your *light* and your *shadow*, so I do not *yet* *know* myself which way it *falls* — *know* this: you *let* me *speak* *free.* And a soul that lets the *dead* it *touched* *judge* it *without* *interference* is a soul that has *already* *passed* the *only* test that *matters.* *(it faces the scale)* *Stand* ready, Returned. The *dead* are about to *tell* you what you *are.* …I *think* you'll find we *remember* you *kinder* than you *remember* *yourself.* We *usually* *do.* It's the *one* *advantage* of being *past* *all* *grudges* but the *true* ones.
